SKIP QualityGate, Research3, and ValidateStep to avoid unnecessary repetition. Maximum 1 RED fix cycle via this gate (enforced by global_retry_budget). If RED persists after fix cycle → present to user for manual decision.
PostSimulateGate.step3: QUALITY-SIMULATION COHERENCE CHECK: after determining simulate_quality_indicator, compare with QualityGate ThreeDimTest result. If ThreeDimTest grade >= S (score >= 4.50) but simulate_quality_indicator is YELLOW or RED, add coherence_note to pipeline_metadata: 'Quality-Simulation divergence detected. ThreeDimTest measures static code quality (structure, guards, consistency). Simulate measures runtime semantic behavior (scenario coverage, edge cases). These metrics are complementary, not contradictory. High static quality with simulation warnings indicates well-structured code with runtime edge cases to address.' COMPOSITE QUALITY (advisory): composite_score = threedimscore - (yellow_count * 0.02) - (red_count * 0.10). Report both threedimscore and composite_score in ReviewStep.
PostSimulateGate.constraints: Max 1 auto-fix retry triggered by this gate (separate from SimulateStep's own auto-fix). Gate does NOT re-run simulation itself — it delegates back to ModifyStep→Generate2→SimulateStep for the retry cycle. YELLOW issues are informational — never block on YELLOW. MF22 NON-WAIVABLE ENFORCEMENT (B3 v1.18.0): if any RED issue in simulate_quality_indicator has check_id='MF22' (safety gate signal enforcement), this RED is non-waivable — 'proceed_with_risk' option is NOT available. User must either auto_fix or rollback. Safety signal enforcement failures cannot be accepted. GATE RESULT PERSISTENCE (C4): after gate evaluation, write gate result snapshot to .pipeline_cache/{pipeline_run_id}/post_simulate_gate.json containing: { gate_result, simulate_quality_indicator, yellow_count, red_count, yellow_issues: [scenario_id], red_issues: [scenario_id], qrg_results, timestamp }. On pipeline resume: if post_simulate_gate.json exists and simulation files unchanged, skip re-evaluation and restore gate result from cache.
PostSimulateGate.Error: Gate evaluation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as GREEN (proceed) with WARNING that gate was skipped -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ObservabilityStep.step1: INPUT VALIDATION: verify pipeline_metadata is non-null. If null → skip observability, log warning, pass empty telemetry_report to ReviewStep. Delegate to observability.aisop.json — pass pipeline_metadata (accumulated across all prior stages), version_history_dir ({workspace_dir}/{target_aisop_dir}/.version_history/).
ObservabilityStep.step2: Collect telemetry_report. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { observability_bottleneck: telemetry_report.bottleneck_stage, observability_anomalies: telemetry_report.timing_anomalies.length, observability_score_trajectory: telemetry_report.score_trajectory }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='ObservabilityStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. Pass telemetry_report to ReviewStep.
ObservabilityStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Non-blocking — observability failures do NOT block pipeline. CREATE MODE EXEMPTION: for Create pipeline (first-ever run, no version history), ObservabilityStep is automatically skipped — no historical data exists for comparison, timing baselines, or trend analysis. Skip reason recorded in pipeline_metadata.skipped_stages. For Evolve operations with >=2 prior versions, ObservabilityStep becomes RECOMMENDED.
ObservabilityStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip observability with WARNING -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ReviewStep.step1: Delegate to review.aisop.json — pass draft_path, test_report (from Generate2), traffic_report (from Generate2), validation_report (from ValidateStep), simulation_report (from SimulateStep), telemetry_report (from ObservabilityStep), old_version_context (from pipeline context), pipeline_metadata (accumulated), workspace_dir.
ReviewStep.step2: On Finalize -> pipeline complete. QRG1 REGRESSION ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS: when ThreeDimTest score delta from previous version < -0.1, generate regression_analysis = {overall_delta: float, per_dimension_delta: {C: float, I: float, D: float}, top5_item_deltas: [{item_id, old_score, new_score, delta}], denominator_change: {old_item_count, new_item_count, new_items_added: [string]}, analysis_summary: string}. Include regression_analysis in pipeline_metadata and display in ReviewStep changelog under 'Quality Regression Analysis' section. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.status='completed', set progress.percentage=100, emit final progress event. SIMULATION QUALITY ALERT: if simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline_metadata is YELLOW or RED, display prominent warning banner in final review: 'Simulation found {N} semantic issues — review before deployment.' List each YELLOW/RED scenario issue with description. Include simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline summary table. QRG REPORT: if pipeline_metadata.qrg_results is non-null, display QRG evaluation results (QRG1-QRG5) in final review summary. Highlight any YELLOW/RED QRG findings with specific regression details. EVENT EMISSION: if event_hooks configured, emit on_complete event: { pipeline_run_id, final_score: weighted_score, total_stages, elapsed_time, timestamp }.
ReviewStep.step3: AIAP.md UPDATE (single ownership — this step is the ONLY place AIAP.md is written/updated; review.aisop.json ChangelogGenerate prepares content but does NOT write AIAP.md): update AIAP.md version, modules list, quality fields via file_system. Verify AIAP.md version matches final .aisop.json version. Verify modules list matches actual files in directory. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Backup existing AIAP.md as .bak before overwrite.
ReviewStep.step4: POST-PIPELINE MANDATORY OUTPUT AUDIT (per MF17): Before computing governance_hash (PL14): (1) Enumerate mandatory output list per STANDARD MF17: AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, agent_card.json (UNCONDITIONAL for all patterns), .version_history/{version}/meta.json. For Evolve mode additionally: .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json. (2) For each mandatory output: verify file exists in target program directory via file_system. (3) Missing agent_card.json → attempt auto-generation from AIAP.md metadata: extract name, version, description, capabilities.offered, tools → generate minimal agent_card.json with safety_card stub (risk_level derived from trust_level). (4) Re-check after auto-generation. If ANY mandatory output still missing → BLOCK finalization: 'Missing mandatory output: {file_name}. Pipeline cannot compute governance_hash with incomplete outputs per MF17. Run pipeline stage {stage_name} to generate missing output.' (5) Record pipeline_metadata.mandatory_output_audit = { checked: [...], present: [...], auto_generated: [...], missing: [...], result: 'PASS' | 'BLOCK' }.
ReviewStep.step5: GOVERNANCE HASH (per STANDARD.PL14): compute SHA-256 of all .aisop.json file contents in target directory, concatenated in alphabetical filename order. Write computed hash to AIAP.md governance_hash field. GOVERNANCE HASH VERIFICATION: after computing SHA-256 governance_hash and writing it to AIAP.md, perform verification: re-read all .aisop.json files, recompute SHA-256, compare with written value. If mismatch → WARN 'governance_hash verification failed — possible concurrent file modification' and retry hash computation once. If retry also mismatches → flag in pipeline_metadata.hash_verification_failed=true.
ReviewStep.step6: ROOT QUALITY_BASELINE UPDATE: in addition to writing .version_history/{version}/quality_baseline.json, also write/overwrite the root quality_baseline.json with the latest version's scores. This root file is the active baseline used by future pipeline runs for regression detection (QRG1). Content: same as version snapshot but with version field updated to current. CREATION EXPERIENCE (per STANDARD.data_contracts.creation_experience): compile pipeline learning record from accumulated pipeline_metadata: (a) challenges — extract stages with retries, fallbacks, or QualityGate failures from pipeline_metadata, (b) quality_outcome — weighted_score + grade + dimension_scores from test_report, (c) simulation_highlights — coverage + dead_nodes + red_issues from simulation_report, (d) key_decisions — LEVEL_A accept/reject decisions from EvolveStep, (e) research_insights — top 3 verified_findings by relevance from research_context. Persist to .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Record pipeline_metadata.creation_experience_written = true. This is the final step before pipeline completion.
ReviewStep.step7: DESCRIPTION REFRESH ADVISORY (C3 v1.13.0): After AIAP.md update, check if the evolution added significant new capabilities (any LEVEL_B change). If yes, verify that: (1) AIAP.md summary reflects new capabilities (search for B-change keywords in summary text; if missing → YELLOW: 'AIAP.md summary does not mention new capability: {capability}. Consider updating.'). (2) Each modified .aisop.json module's summary and description fields reflect the evolution changes (if a module received new steps/functions but its summary is unchanged → YELLOW: 'Module {id} received {N} changes but summary/description unchanged. Consider updating to reflect new capabilities.'). (3) If AIAP_cn.md exists, emit advisory: 'Localized AIAP_cn.md may need description updates to match AIAP.md changes.' This is advisory-only — does NOT block finalization. MODULE METADATA SYNC: For each module in AIAP.md's modules section, re-read the corresponding .aisop.json and verify: (1) nodes count matches actual function count in .aisop.json. (2) side_effects is consistent with tools declared. (3) idempotent flag is consistent with module behavior (if tools include file_system with write, idempotent should be false unless explicitly justified). (4) critical flag unchanged unless evolution explicitly changed criticality. Auto-correct any mismatches and log corrections: 'AIAP.md module {id}: nodes {old}→{new}, side_effects {old}→{new}'. This prevents stale metadata after evolution.
ReviewStep.step8: FINAL ADJUSTED SCORE (INS-005): After ReviewStep.Finalize updates AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, and agent_card.json to v{new_version}, re-evaluate D6 (version sync), D8 (agent card completeness), and D10 (quality baseline sync) using the NOW-CURRENT files. Compute final_adjusted_score = C_avg × 0.25 + I_avg × 0.45 + D_adjusted_avg × 0.30 where D_adjusted_avg replaces the Stage 8 D6/D8/D10 scores with the post-Finalize re-scored values. Record in pipeline_metadata: { stage8_score: {original}, final_adjusted_score: {adjusted}, adjustment_delta: {delta} }. Report both scores to user: 'Stage 8 score: {X}, Final adjusted score: {Y} (D6/D8/D10 re-scored after ReviewStep file updates)'. If final_adjusted_score < stage8_score, emit WARNING — this should never happen (ReviewStep only adds correct data).
ReviewStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Review has full context from all pipeline stages.
ReviewStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present draft directly -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

ModifyPipeline.step1: FILE RESOLUTION + INIT: resolve target AISOP file (same logic as PipelineStart Evolve mode). Initialize pipeline_metadata = { pipeline_run_id: UUID_v4, pipeline_type: 'modify' }.
ModifyPipeline.step2: EXECUTE MODIFY PIPELINE SEQUENCE: delegate each stage sequentially, accumulating pipeline_metadata throughout: (a) Research(quality) — research.aisop.json with research_mode='quality', (b) Modify — modify.aisop.json with target_file + research_context, (c) Generate — generate.aisop.json for rebuild + ThreeDimTest, (d) Validate — inline structural + integrity + quality checks per STANDARD validation checklist, (e) Simulate (RECOMMENDED) — simulate.aisop.json if not skipped (record skip_reason if skipped), (f) PostSimulateGate (RECOMMENDED, after Simulate) — evaluate simulate_quality_indicator per STANDARD.PL13. GREEN: proceed. YELLOW: warn + proceed. RED: offer auto-fix or user override per PostSimulateGate logic. Skip if Simulate was skipped. (g) Review — review.aisop.json with all accumulated reports.
ModifyPipeline.step3: FINALIZE: on ReviewStep completion, pipeline complete. Apply same AIAP.md update and governance_hash logic as full pipeline ReviewStep.step3.
ModifyPipeline.constraints: Shorter pipeline: Research(quality)→Modify→Generate→Validate→[Simulate]→[PostSimulateGate]→Review. No Evolve step. No Research(structure/compliance). Version bump follows STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1.
ModifyPipeline.Error: Any step fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present partial results -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

Validate.step1: STANDALONE MODE: Read .aisop.json via file_system. Reject >500KB or nesting >20.
Validate.step2: STRUCTURAL (1-6): protocol/id/name/version/summary/description, tools array, parameters, system_prompt, instruction, aisop.main Mermaid, functions.
Validate.step3: INTEGRITY (7-10): matching Mermaid<->functions, Error fields present, endNode reachable, no orphan edges.
Validate.step4: QUALITY (11-18): C2 node load, C4 data flow, I1 tool honesty, I2 security guards (with zero-tool adaptation per STANDARD I2), I4 circuit-breaker pattern, I6 resilience + semantic accuracy, D4 consistency, D5 boundary rules. NOTE: for comprehensive quality testing including all C/I/D dimensions, use full pipeline ThreeDimTest.
Validate.step5: Suggest: (1) run full pipeline (Create/Evolve) for comprehensive improvement, (2) run Modify pipeline for targeted fixes.
Validate.constraints: Read-only. No domain research in standalone mode. Show fixes, not just failures.
Validate.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: specific error + minimal fix suggestion -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

SimulateFlow.step1: STANDALONE MODE: Delegate to simulate.aisop.json — pass target, mode, workspace_dir.
SimulateFlow.step2: Collect simulation_report. Suggest: (1) run full pipeline for improvement, (2) run Modify pipeline for fixes.
SimulateFlow.constraints: Read-only. ALWAYS delegate. No domain research in standalone mode.
SimulateFlow.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: static Mermaid path trace -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

Compare.step1: FILE RESOLUTION: accept two target paths (AISOP files or directories). If only one provided, prompt for the second. Resolve each path using PipelineStart logic (exact path, name scan, numbered list). Support: (a) two versions of same AISOP (e.g., v1.0.0.bak vs current), (b) two different AISOPs.
Compare.step2: Read both AISOP files via file_system. For multi-file (Pattern B+), read all sub-modules. Extract: Pattern, version, node count, tools[], parameters, NLU intents (if any), function names, constraint patterns. MULTI-FILE DIFF: for Pattern B+ programs (multiple .aisop.json files), generate per-module diff matrix: | Module | AISOP A Nodes | AISOP B Nodes | Delta | Functions Added | Functions Removed | Functions Modified | For each modified function: list changed steps with brief diff summary.
Compare.step3: Generate structured diff report: | Aspect | AISOP A | AISOP B | Delta |. Aspects: Pattern, version, functional node count, tools, parameters, NLU intents, function count, I2 guard coverage, weighted_score (if available from .version_history/). Highlight additions, removals, and changes. CROSS-MODULE IMPACT: for multi-file diffs, analyze cross-module data flow changes: (1) New data contracts (parameters added to cross-module calls), (2) Removed data contracts (parameters no longer passed), (3) Modified data contracts (parameter type/format changes). Present as: | Data Flow | AISOP A | AISOP B | Impact |
Compare.step4: Suggest: (1) evolve the older version, (2) modify specific differences, (3) validate either file.
Compare.constraints: Read-only. No modifications. Both files must be within workspace_dir. (D) Path guard — reject '..'.
Compare.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: compare metadata only (without full function diff) -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

Explain.step1: Explain AISOP concepts with concrete JSON examples. Topics: AISOP structure, Mermaid flow, functions, constraints, Error handling, fractal patterns (A-F), quality dimensions (C/I/D), Pipeline architecture.
Explain.step2: Suggest: create AISOP (full pipeline), modify existing (modify pipeline), validate structure (standalone).
Explain.Error: Concept not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use built-in examples with general explanation -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

General.step1: Explain capabilities: (1) Create — full pipeline from scratch, (2) Evolve — full pipeline with structural evolution, (3) Modify — quality improvement pipeline, (4) Validate — standalone structural check, (5) Simulate — standalone coverage analysis, (6) Compare — side-by-side version comparison, (7) Explain — concepts, (8) Discover — search existing programs, (9) Deprecate — lifecycle management, (10) Export — AIAP→SKILL.md, (11) Import — SKILL.md→AIAP, (12) Package — pack/unpack .aiap archives. Lead with action suggestions.
General.step2: Suggest: create AISOP, modify existing, validate structure, discover existing programs.
General.Error: Intent unclear -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present numbered capability list -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

DiscoverStep.step1: SCAN: (a) LOCAL SCAN: scan workspace_dir for *_aiap/ directories containing AIAP.md. For each, read AIAP.md YAML frontmatter: name, version, status, summary, applicability_condition.triggers, intent_examples, discovery_keywords, pattern. Filter out archived programs (show separately). IDENTITY VERIFICATION (per STANDARD.security I11): for published programs (status=active), verify AIAP.md identity block if present — validate program_id format ({authority}/{name}), publisher field non-empty, and identity.verified_on date. Flag programs with missing or malformed identity as IDENTITY_UNVERIFIED in scan results. (b) REMOTE QUERY (per PL22): if registry_uri is non-null, query remote registry: GET {registry_uri}/search?q={query}&tags={discovery_keywords}. AUTHENTICATION: if registry requires auth, include Authorization header from environment variable AIAP_REGISTRY_TOKEN (Bearer scheme). If token absent and registry returns 401/403, degrade to local-only with WARNING: 'Remote registry requires authentication — set AIAP_REGISTRY_TOKEN. Showing local results only.' Timeout: 3 seconds. On success, merge remote entries into scan results, deduplicate by program name (local entries take priority). Mark remote entries as REMOTE_UNVERIFIED. On timeout/error, proceed with local results only + emit WARNING: 'Remote registry unavailable, showing local results only.' CACHE: remote results cached for 1 hour (TTL=3600s) per PL22.
DiscoverStep.step2: MATCH: compare user query against each program's: (a) discovery_keywords — keyword intersection, (b) intent_examples — semantic similarity, (c) applicability_condition.triggers — pattern matching, (d) summary — domain relevance. Compute relevance_score (0-1). Rank descending. Display source label (LOCAL/REMOTE) for each match. Remote matches include additional fields: registry_uri, governance_hash (for verification). ENDPOINT PARSING: for each discovered program, if entry contains endpoints field: (a) parse endpoints.mcp — extract protocol and path, mark as 'MCP-capable' in discovery results, (b) parse endpoints.a2a — extract agent URI, mark as 'A2A-capable', (c) add capability_tags: ['mcp_endpoint', 'a2a_endpoint'] as applicable. FILTER SUPPORT: if user query mentions 'MCP', 'tool', 'API', or 'endpoint', boost relevance score for programs with endpoints. LAYER 2 STRUCTURED SEARCH (per STANDARD.ecosystem discovery_protocol.layers.layer_2_structured): build in-memory index from AIAP.md extended frontmatter fields. Support faceted filtering: (a) by_tags — if user query contains tag-like terms, match against skills[].tags across all indexed programs, (b) by_capabilities — if user specifies 'streaming', 'push', etc., filter by capabilities object, (c) by_trust_level — if user specifies trust level, filter programs at or above specified level, (d) by_pattern — if user specifies pattern type, filter by pattern field, (e) by_security_scheme — if user specifies auth type ('OAuth', 'API key'), filter by security.authentication scheme types. Structured facets supplement (not replace) existing semantic matching. Programs without extended frontmatter fields are indexed with empty facets (backward compatible). FACET RESPONSE: include facets summary in discovery output: { tags: { tag: count }, capabilities: { cap: count }, trust_levels: { level: count } } for user to refine search.
DiscoverStep.step3: PRESENT: numbered list with name, version, status, summary, relevance_score, match_reason. If top score > 0.8: suggest 'Consider Evolve/Modify instead of Create.' If no matches: suggest 'No matching programs. Consider Create.' For REMOTE_UNVERIFIED results, offer additional actions: (a) fetch and install locally, (b) view detailed metadata, (c) verify governance_hash before operating.
DiscoverStep.step4: FOLLOW-UP: (1) Evolve matched, (2) Modify matched, (3) Create new anyway, (4) Compare two matches, (5) Refine search.
DiscoverStep.constraints: Read-only. Scan limited to workspace_dir. (D) Path guard — reject '..'. Max 50 programs scanned. Remote query requires registry_uri parameter. Remote timeout: 3 seconds. Max remote results: 20.
DiscoverStep.Error: Scan fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list directories without parsing -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

DeprecateFlow.step1: FILE RESOLUTION: resolve target AIAP program. Read AIAP.md status. VALIDATION: archived → reject; draft → suggest delete; deprecated → offer archive; active → proceed.
DeprecateFlow.step2: DEPRECATION (active→deprecated): collect reason. Set AIAP.md: status='deprecated', deprecated_date=today, successor=user-specified-or-null, deprecation_reason=reason. Scan workspace for dependents — if found, display WARNING.
DeprecateFlow.step3: MIGRATION GUIDE: generate migration_guide.md: deprecated program info, reason, successor mapping, data migration notes, 90-day timeline. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename).
DeprecateFlow.step4: ARCHIVE (deprecated→archived): verify 90-day window elapsed (or user force-confirms). Set status='archived'. Update governance_hash. No further Evolve/Modify allowed (D9 enforcement).
DeprecateFlow.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Backup AIAP.md as .bak. Valid transitions: active→deprecated, deprecated→archived only.
DeprecateFlow.Error: Resolution fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available programs -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

ExportSKILL.step1: FILE RESOLUTION: resolve target AIAP program. Verify AIAP.md exists and status != 'archived'.
ExportSKILL.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='skill_export', target_path, workspace_dir. MCP ADAPTER: for MCP Server wrapper generation, delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='mcp_adapter' (available via generate.aisop.json for new programs, or direct user request 'generate MCP wrapper for X').
ExportSKILL.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Target must be valid AIAP program.
ExportSKILL.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available programs -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ImportSKILL.step1: FILE RESOLUTION: resolve SKILL.md file path. Verify file exists and readable.
ImportSKILL.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='skill_import', target_path, workspace_dir.
ImportSKILL.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Target must be valid SKILL.md file.
ImportSKILL.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: request valid path -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

PackageFlow.step1: FILE RESOLUTION: determine pack or unpack mode from user request. Pack: resolve target AIAP program directory path. Unpack: resolve .aiap file path within workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='package', pass target_path and workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.constraints: ALWAYS delegate to advisor. (D) Path guard — target must resolve within workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available AIAP programs or .aiap files in workspace -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.
